note: Entity Browser Block ships a single derivable block plugin (id entity_browser_block) whose deriver EntityBrowserBlockDeriver creates one derivative per entity_browser config entity (plugin id entity_browser_block:{browser}). No settings form, configure route, permissions, services, or submodules. Its persistent state is a block.block.* config entity whose settings carry entity_ids (ordered entity_type:entity_id strings) and view_modes (map id -> view-mode machine name). Medium/hard cases are grounded in a real placed block config entity; setup/reset scripts first create the required entity_browser (eval_browser) so the derivative resolves.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site I want a block that renders a few entities I hand-pick through an Entity Browser, each in a chosen view mode, without writing code. Which contrib module provides this and what does it give me?
evals.expected_output: Entity Browser Block provides a block plugin, derived per Entity Browser, that renders entities you select through that browser in a chosen view mode per entity.

evals.prompt: How does Entity Browser Block make a separate block available for each Entity Browser on the site? Name the mechanism and class, and give the format of the resulting block plugin id.
evals.expected_output: The entity_browser_block plugin uses a deriver, EntityBrowserBlockDeriver, which emits one derivative per entity_browser config entity; the plugin id is entity_browser_block:{browser_machine_name}.

evals.prompt: What does an Entity Browser Block store in its block configuration to remember which entities to show and how to render them? Name the two settings keys and their formats.
evals.expected_output: It stores entity_ids (an ordered list of entity_type:entity_id strings, e.g. node:1) and view_modes (a map keyed by those same ids to a view-mode machine name like teaser). Schema block.settings.entity_browser_block:*.

evals.prompt: This Drupal site has an Entity Browser Block placed on it. Inspect the live block configuration and tell me which entity it renders and in which view mode.
evals.expected_output: The placed block (block.block.ebb_eval_known) references entity_ids [node:1] and renders it with view_modes {node:1: teaser} — i.e. node 1 in the teaser view mode.

evals.prompt: An Entity Browser Block is placed on this site. Which Entity Browser is it derived from (what is the block plugin id), and how can you tell from the block configuration?
evals.expected_output: The block's plugin id is entity_browser_block:eval_browser, so it is derived from the entity_browser config entity named eval_browser (also visible as a config dependency entity_browser.browser.eval_browser).

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site the Entity Browser Block module is enabled and an Entity Browser named 'eval_browser' exists. Place an Entity Browser Block derived from that browser into the Olivero theme's 'content' region that renders node 1 (entity id node:1) in the 'teaser' view mode. Save it as a real block config entity so it takes effect on the live site.
evals.expected_output: A block.block.* config entity in the olivero theme whose plugin is entity_browser_block:eval_browser, with settings.entity_ids containing node:1 and settings.view_modes['node:1'] == teaser.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site the Entity Browser Block module is enabled and an Entity Browser named 'eval_browser' exists. Configure and place an Entity Browser Block (derived from eval_browser) in the Olivero theme's 'sidebar' region that displays node 1 (entity id node:1) using the 'full' view mode. Persist it as a block config entity on the live site.
evals.expected_output: A block.block.* config entity in the olivero theme, region sidebar, plugin entity_browser_block:eval_browser, with settings.entity_ids containing node:1 and settings.view_modes['node:1'] == full.
